era_context: Shalmaneser III's reign in Assyria continues. The Levantine states remain under Assyrian tributary control. In Greece, the Archaic period is progressing with early colonization efforts. Western Zhou in China is in its final years before collapse. Egypt remains fragmented under the late 21st Dynasty.

events.title: Shalmaneser III's Levantine campaigns
events.description: Shalmaneser III continued his systematic military campaigns into the Levant, maintaining Assyrian tributary control and extracting resources from subject states. These campaigns, documented in royal inscriptions, were part of the annual military program that defined his reign and established Assyrian imperial dominance in the Near East.
events.certainty_note: Shalmaneser III's campaigns are well-attested; specific year assignment follows Assyrian chronology with ±2 year uncertainty.

disconfirming_evidence: No specific events of 816 BCE are individually documented. The year is reconstructed from the broader pattern of Shalmaneser III's reign. Chronological uncertainty in Assyrian dating means this year could be off by 1–2 years in alternative chronological systems.
historiographic_note: 816 BCE is not individually attested in surviving sources. The year is assigned significance only through its position within Shalmaneser III's documented reign. This illustrates the fundamental limitation of year-by-year ancient history: most years leave no discrete trace. The surviving record is dominated by Assyrian royal propaganda; other regions and perspectives are essentially absent.

graph_edges.note: Continuation of Shalmaneser III's sustained campaign pattern.
